class.name: AbilitySystemEffect
class.brief_description: Resource defining modifications to attributes and tags.
class.description: [AbilitySystemEffect] defines a formal package of attribute and tag modifications. [b]Architectural Note:[/b] Effects are registered in the source actor's [AbilitySystemAbilityContainer] as part of an archetype contract (blueprint). For example, a Sniper character has a "Single Shot" effect in its container, which is then instantiated into a [AbilitySystemEffectSpec] and applied to targets.

method.name: add_modifier
method.description: Adds a new attribute modifier to the effect.

method.name: get_modifier_attribute
method.description: Returns the attribute name modified by the modifier at the given index.

method.name: get_modifier_count
method.description: Returns the number of attribute modifiers in this effect.

method.name: get_modifier_custom_magnitude
method.description: Returns the custom magnitude calculation for the modifier at the given index.

method.name: get_modifier_magnitude
method.description: Returns the magnitude value for the modifier at the given index.

method.name: get_modifier_operation
method.description: Returns the operation type for the modifier at the given index.

members.member: Tags that are blocked while this effect is active.
List of [AbilitySystemCue] resources to be executed directly when this effect is applied or removed.
The duration value (if policy is DURATION).
How the effect duration is handled (INSTANT, DURATION, INFINITE).
Unique name for this effect resource.
Unique tag identifying this effect, also used to trigger cues.
If [code]true[/code], a periodic tick fires immediately when the effect is first applied.
Tags granted to the actor while this effect is active.
The number of attribute modifiers in this effect.
If greater than [code]0[/code], the effect's modifiers are applied as instant ticks every [member period] seconds to the base attribute value. Used for Damage-over-Time or Heal-over-Time effects.
Tags removed from the actor when this effect is applied.
Defines behavior when a duplicate effect is applied on the same target. See [enum StackingPolicy].

constants.constant: Effect is applied instantly and has no duration.
Effect stays active for a specified duration.
Effect stays active until explicitly removed.
Each application is a fully independent effect instance. Default behavior.
The new application replaces the old one, resetting its timer. Ideal for buffs that should not stack, e.g. a speed boost.
A [code]stack_count[/code] is incremented and the modifier magnitude is multiplied by it. Ideal for stacking damage buffs.
The remaining duration is extended by the new application's full duration. Ideal for refreshable HoT/DoT effects.
Value is added to the attribute.
Value is multiplied with the attribute.
Value is divided from the attribute.
Value replaces the current attribute value.
